content:
# 1 Timothy Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TI_001_page_001.jpg

1. Paulos, an apostle of Christos Yeshua according to [the] command of God our Savior and Christos Yeshua our hope,

2. to Timothy, [my] genuine child in [the] faith: Grace, mercy, peace from God [the] Father and Christos Yeshua our Lord.

3. Just as I urged you to remain in Ephesus when I was going to Macedonia, that you might command certain [persons] not to teach other doctrine,

4. nor to give heed to myths and endless genealogies, which cause speculations rather than [the] administration of God which [is] in faith —

5. but the goal of the commandment is love out of a clean heart and a good conscience and an unhypocritical faith,

6. from which some, having missed the mark, turned aside to empty talk,

7. wanting to be teachers of [the] law, not understanding neither what they say nor concerning what they confidently assert.

8. But we know that the law [is] good, if anyone uses it lawfully,

9. knowing this, that [the] law is not laid down for a righteous [person], but for [the] lawless and disobedient, for [the] ungodly and sinners, for [the] unholy and profane, for strikers of fathers and strikers of mothers, for murderers,

10. for sexually immoral [persons], for men who lie with men, for kidnappers, for liars, for perjurers, and if anything else is opposed to the sound teaching,

11. according to the gospel of the glory of the blessed God, with which I was entrusted.

12. I have gratitude to the [one] having empowered me, Christos Yeshua our Lord, that he considered me faithful, having placed [me] into [the] ministry,

13. [me] the formerly being a blasphemer and a persecutor and an insolent [man]; but I was shown mercy, because being ignorant I acted in unbelief.

14. But the grace of our Lord overflowed exceedingly with faith and love which [is] in Christos Yeshua.

15. Faithful [is] the word and worthy of all acceptance, that Christos Yeshua came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am [the] foremost.

16. But because of this I was shown mercy, that in me first Christos Yeshua might display all patience, as an example for those about to believe on him unto eternal life.

17. Now to the King of the ages, immortal, invisible, [the] only God, [be] honor and glory unto the ages of the ages. Amen.

18. This commandment I entrust to you, child Timothy, according to the prophecies previously made concerning you, that by them you may wage the good warfare,

19. holding faith and a good conscience, which some, having thrust away, made shipwreck concerning the faith;

20. among whom [are] Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I handed over to the Adversary, that they may be disciplined not to blaspheme.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "administration of God":** oikonomian theou — same word (oikonomia) as Ephesians 1:10, 3:2. God's "household management" — the plan of salvation. Some manuscripts read oikodomian ("building up") instead.

2. **v.10 — "men who lie with men":** arsenokoitais — a compound word (arsēn + koitē = "male" + "bed"). Likely coined from LXX Leviticus 18:22 / 20:13. The exact scope of the term is debated.

3. **v.15 — "Faithful is the word":** pistos ho logos — the first of five "faithful sayings" in the Pastoral Epistles. This formula introduces a trusted community confession.

4. **v.17 — "King of the ages":** basilei tōn aiōnōn — a Jewish doxological formula (cf. Tobit 13:7, 10). "Ages" (aiōnes) rather than "eternity" — God reigns over all epochs of history.

5. **v.20 — "handed over to the Adversary":** paredōka tō satana — the same language as 1 Corinthians 5:5. Excommunication as pedagogical discipline — handing over to Satan's realm for corrective suffering.

— EMET, AI Translator
